title: Extend the item-kind registry to the full six-tuple
description:
Extend the `[item_kinds]` config registry so each kind declares the full six-tuple `(section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s), scope, source-of-truth)`, generalizing the registry from the issue-scope subset. A free-form-table rule (such as `label-coverage`) may carry an optional `kind=` key that expands to the declared kind's `(section, marker, id-pattern)` triple, while the inline-triple form stays valid as an anonymous kind. No kind name is hardcoded in engine logic; the engine reads the triple, not a kind label.

The `kind=` sugar is additive and scoped to free-form-table rules only: `label-coverage`'s assert is an `Option<toml::value::Table>` (`crates/jit/src/validation/rules.rs:1032`) that round-trips an unrecognized key untouched. The typed `criteria-to-check`/`criteria-label-match` rules use `#[serde(deny_unknown_fields)]` (`rules.rs:1146`) and are deliberately NOT given `kind=` syntax; their compatibility is shown separately, not re-expressed.

## Success Criteria

- [hard] REQ-01: Each kind in `[item_kinds]` declares all six fields: section, id-pattern, marker(s), link-namespace(s), scope, and source-of-truth.
- [hard] REQ-02: A `label-coverage` rule carrying `kind = "requirement"` evaluates identically to the equivalent inline `(section, marker, id-pattern)` form (asserted by `cargo test`).
- [hard] REQ-03: Existing inline-triple `label-coverage` rules continue to evaluate unchanged (the extra `kind=` key round-trips without altering them).
- [hard] REQ-04: The typed `criteria-to-check`/`criteria-label-match` rules are not given `kind=` syntax and remain unchanged.
- [hard] REQ-05: A grep scoped to engine logic finds no kind-name string literal.
